heroHeadline: Drone Strikes Kill 44+ Civilians in Darfur and Kordofan; SAF-RSF Stalemate Deepens Into Fourth Year
heroSubtitle: Drone strikes on April 16 killed at least 44 civilians — 24+ in Umm Dukhun (Central Darfur) and 9 at El Wahda market in El Fula (West Kordofan, including 6 children) — as the Sudan civil war grinds into its fourth year with neither SAF nor RSF achieving decisive victory; the US Treasury sanctions on Colombian mercenary recruiters (April 18) marked the first direct US action against RSF foreign fighter networks, while the Berlin Conference's €1.3 billion in pledges remains largely undisbursed with only 16% of the $2.9B UN 2026 appeal funded
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle
